definition.meaning: One who builds, constructs houses or other structures

definition.origin: From Old English byldan "to construct a house", verb form of bold "house"

definition.meaning: To construct a house, to build
definition.origin: Verb form of bold "house", from Proto-Germanic *buthla- "building, house"
definition.etymology: From PIE *bhu- "to dwell", from root *bheue- "to be, exist, grow"

definition.cognates: Old Saxon bodl "house", Old Frisian bodel "building, house"

definition.meaning: To be, exist, grow; to dwell
definition.note: Root of build, booth, bower, and related dwelling concepts

definition.meaning: tógálaí: builder (one who raises/erects); foirgneoir: builder, constructor
definition.origin: tógálaí from tóg "to raise, lift, build"

definition.meaning: Craftsman, builder, carpenter; also means "free" (a free craftsman)
definition.origin: From Old Irish saer "craftsman, wright". Gobán Saor = legendary master builder of 7th century Ireland
definition.note: Saor carries dual meaning of "craftsman" and "free person" — builders were freemen

definition.meaning: costruttore: builder, constructor; muratore: mason, bricklayer
definition.origin: costruttore from Latin construere "to pile up, build"
